title: Vercte's Satchels
body:
Vercte's Satchels adds one item: the Satchel. The Satchel is an item that unlocks six extra slots in your inventory, located below the hotbar. Once equipped, its contents can be swapped with the first six slots1 of your hotbar using a keybind (default `V`).

This can be very useful! Use it to store extra building blocks, tools, clean up your main hotbar, or just use it as an inventory extension—because it is one. The Satchel also integrates itself seamlessly into vanilla container UIs, as well as working with Modded UIs.2

I made this mod because I always run out of hotbar space when doing tasks around my base, compared to running out of inventory space. It adds an extra layer of inventory management through deciding the layout of your satchel and hotbar, and when combined with bundles,3 it makes inventory management more fun to figure out compared to throwing everything in a backpack or shulker. It's also dyeable, but I'll let you figure out how that looks for yourself :) ### Controls - `V`1 to toggle the Satchel Hotbar - `shift+[hotbar key]`4 to swap an item into the Satchel Hotbar ### Contributors - **RememberThePorter** (CreepersNeedHugs): French Localization - **Want to translate?** Ask me on the [Discord](https://discord.gg/XRJqAdDGvp), or open a PR on the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/vercte/satchels/) if you have the know-how! ### Compatiblity - **JEI/EMI**: Exclusion zones when the vanilla satchel slot is open (pushes ingredients away) - **Raised**: Animation goes fully off screen - **Curios**: Replaces vanilla slot with the curios slot ### Similar Mods While my original mod concept and item names were originally found by me, I did find some very similar mods in both name and concept. I still tried to make mine original with its own quirks, though :) - Scout: https://modrinth.com/mod/scout - Satchels: https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/satchels 1 configurable in the Controls menu
2 configurable in the Common Config satchels-common.toml (the list must be the same on the Client and Server!)
3 this is where I recommend using this with Vanilla Backport's bundles
4 configurable in the Client Config satchels-client.toml
